input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known breaking change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `adjustResize` in favor of the new Edge-to-Edge / WindowInsets API starting with Android 15.

In .NET 10 (MAUI), the recommended approach is to use the new `KeyboardAcceleratorsChanged` or more specifically, the Android edge-to-edge support. Let me think about what the actual solution is.

In .NET MAUI for .NET 10, the shift is toward using Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` API. The `adjustResize` soft input mode was deprecated in Android API 30+ and MAUI .NET 10 moved to edge-to-edge by default on Android.


The fix requires handling window insets properly rather than relying on the deprecated `adjustResize` mode. In .NET 10 MAUI, you'd typically use `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` to manage keyboard insets, or leverage MAUI's built-in keyboard-aware layout support for Android edge-to-edge compatibility. Setting up a keyboard insets listener on Android to detect when the IME appears and adjust the view's bottom padding accordingly...

Actually, for .NET 10 MAUI there's probably a cleaner cross-platform solution using layout containers and the built-in insets handling, so let me think through the most practical approach here.
